findings.issue: The `HiveSyncConfig::default()` documentation example was converted to an ignored doctest even though it is a pure, zero-I/O invariant check over public API defaults. This unnecessarily removes executable coverage for the documented default values; a future change to the defaults can now drift from the docs without `cargo test --doc -p services` catching it.
findings.remediation: Change the fence back to a live Rust doctest and add the missing public import: `/// ```rust`, then `/// use services::services::hive_sync::HiveSyncConfig;`, followed by the existing assertions. This keeps the doctest compiling and running without any runtime setup.

findings.issue: The `NodeApiKeyError` to `NodeError` conversion example was ignored even though it is a self-contained mapping assertion over public types. The only problem is the stale `crate::` path, so ignoring it carries forward avoidable documentation rot and drops a useful regression check for the error mapping.
findings.remediation: Replace the ignored fence with a live Rust doctest and use external crate paths: `use remote::db::node_api_keys::NodeApiKeyError; use remote::nodes::NodeError; let api_err = NodeApiKeyError::NotFound; let node_err: NodeError = api_err.into(); assert!(matches!(node_err, NodeError::ApiKeyNotFound));`.

findings.issue: The `SwarmProjectError` to `NodeError` conversion example was also silenced with `ignore` despite being a zero-I/O assertion that can compile and run with corrected public paths. This weakens doctest validation for a documented error-mapping contract instead of fixing the stale imports at source level.
findings.remediation: Replace the ignored fence with a live Rust doctest using public paths: `use remote::db::swarm_projects::SwarmProjectError; use remote::nodes::NodeError; let node_err: NodeError = SwarmProjectError::NotFound.into(); assert!(matches!(node_err, NodeError::ProjectNotInHive));`.
